name: repo-incoming-attachments

systems.name: opencode daemon
systems.description: User-level daemon serving session, attachments, and mcp dispatch.

containers.name: opencode bun daemon
containers.description: Hosts upload route, tool dispatcher, mcp client manager.

containers.name: docxmcp container
containers.description: Stateless docx compute. Sees only /state mount.
containers.boundaryRule: no host repo / HOME mount; only /state

containers.name: repo working tree
containers.description: Per-session project root. Holds incoming/ and bundles co-resident with originals.

components.name: Upload HTTP route
components.description: Receives POST upload, atomic-writes to incoming/, calls IncomingHistory.appendUpload.

components.name: history journal
components.description: jsonl append, tail, currentSha256 lookup with stat-based drift detection.

components.name: mcp tool dispatcher
components.description: Stage repo-path inputs into /state, invokes mcp tool, publishes outputs back to incoming/<stem>/. sha-keyed cache check before tool call.

components.name: convertMcpTool wrapper
components.description: Wraps mcp tool execute with dispatcher; substitutes repo paths with staging paths in args; reverse maps in result paths.

components.name: tool post-write history hook
components.description: On tool return, if any incoming/** path was written, call IncomingHistory.appendToolWrite.

relationships.purpose: append bundle-published event for <stem>/ contents and re-hash original
append tool:<name> event after every Edit/Write/Bash that touches incoming/**

deploymentNotes: C-DAEMON runs as user-level systemd unit. C-MCP-DOCXMCP is spawned by daemon via stdio (StdioClientTransport with command='/usr/bin/docker run -i --rm ... docxmcp:latest'). Docker mount narrow: only ~/.local/state/<staging-area>:/state.
